fix: adaptive table sizing in RouteJumpTable to prevent startup crash (#1031) (#1033)

The perfect hash seed search failed with 84 routes in a 256-slot table
because the birthday-problem probability makes a collision-free seed
astronomically rare (~1 in 800K per attempt) at that load factor.

Changes:
- Start table at 4x route count instead of 2x (better birthday-problem
  odds for typical route counts)
- Adaptively double table size up to 16x if seed search exhausts 100k
  attempts at current size
- Reuse occupied[] array across seed attempts via Array.Clear
- Log when table upsizing occurs for diagnostics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: custom BMW JSON serializer/deserializer (issue #1003) (#1032)

* feat: custom BMW JSON serializer/deserializer (issue #1003)

Implement metadata-driven binary ↔ JSON transcoder that eliminates
System.Text.Json from entity API hot paths:

BmwJsonWriter: reads BSO1 binary row spans via SpanReader and emits
UTF-8 JSON directly to Stream. Precomputed JsonFieldFragment[] caches
property-name tokens at startup. Supports single entity and batch list
overloads. Uses Utf8Formatter for zero-allocation number formatting
and fast-path JSON string escaping.

BmwJsonReader: single-pass UTF-8 JSON scanner with precomputed
ordinal lookup table (JsonPropertyLookup[]) for property name matching.
Parses JSON values into per-field binary-encoded slots, then assembles
BSO1 binary row in ordinal order. Supports create and merge (PATCH)
modes. Unknown properties ignored, case-insensitive name matching.

Integration:
- BinaryApiHandlers.WriteEntityResponse/WriteListResponse now use
  BmwJsonWriter (binary → JSON) instead of Utf8JsonWriter
- BinaryApiHandlers.ReadEntityFromRequest now uses BmwJsonReader
  (JSON → binary) instead of JsonDocument.ParseAsync
- DeltaApiHandlers PATCH response uses BmwJsonWriter
- Cached fragment/lookup tables alongside existing plan cache

Both classes handle all WireFieldTypes: Bool, integers, floats, Decimal,
String (with escape handling), Guid, DateTime, DateOnly, TimeOnly,
DateTimeOffset, TimeSpan, Identifier, Enum.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: raw binary API fast path — skip CLR materialisation (issue #1003)

Add IRawBinaryProvider interface and implement on WalDataProvider to
return raw BSO1 binary payloads directly from WAL storage without
deserialising into CLR objects.

Wire up GetHandler and ListHandler with a fast path that checks:
- Client wants JSON (Accept header)
- Raw binary provider is available
- No Object-type fields in the FieldPlan (unsupported by BmwJsonWriter)

When all conditions are met, binary → JSON transcoding happens directly
via BmwJsonWriter with zero intermediate CLR object allocation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: extract real upload URL from Steve resource actions

The ?action=upload endpoint is not registered in Steve for the
backingimage backend (Harvester v1.2+). Steve returns 403
'no such action upload' regardless of the URL prefix used.

The correct upload URL is embedded in the VirtualMachineImage
resource's 'actions' map (Steve embeds action URLs in each resource
object). Extract it from there when Initialized is detected, logging
all available actions for visibility.

If no upload-related action is found in the resource (actions map is
empty or contains no 'upload' key), fall back to the constructed URL
so the old behaviour is preserved.

Also refactor the Python3 upload call to accept a single full URL
instead of separate server + path arguments, so any URL source works.

commit.message:
fix: handle init/fini array base address and external TLS symbols

- Fix init_array/fini_array entries not being adjusted by base address for system libraries (glibc)
  - Added heuristic detection: if entry < MemSize, treat as virtual address and add base
  - Applies to both initialization and uninitialization phases
  - Enables calling math functions (cos, sin, sqrt, pow, fabs) from libm.so.6

- Fix TPOFF64 relocations for libraries without PT_TLS segment
  - Support external TLS symbol resolution (e.g., libm referencing libc's errno)
  - Gracefully handle libraries without TLS by resolving symbols from other modules
  - Write 0 for unresolvable weak symbols to prevent crashes

- Add panic recovery in Unload() for fini functions
  - System library fini functions may expect runtime state not provided by this minimal loader
  - Catch panics to allow cleanup to continue even if individual fini functions fail
  - Prevents crash during library cleanup (known limitation documented)

Updates AUDIT.md to mark HIGH-01 as partially resolved with actual progress made.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
security: fix 5 vulnerabilities found in audit

- CRITICAL (CORS): Replace wildcard allow_origins+allow_credentials=True
  combination with safe defaults. When ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var is not
  set, CORS is now restricted to same-origin only with a startup warning.
  allow_methods and allow_headers are now explicit allowlists instead of
  wildcard '*'. Fixes potential CSRF via credentialed cross-origin requests.

- HIGH (Error leakage): Remove str(e) from HTTP responses to prevent
  internal implementation details from being sent to clients. Errors are
  now logged server-side; clients receive a generic 500 message.
  Affects: diagnose_controller.py and /api/v1/cluster/status endpoint.

- MEDIUM (Input validation): Add Kubernetes-compliant regex validation for
  pod_name path parameter and namespace body field (lowercase alphanumeric
  + hyphens, max 253 chars). Invalid values return HTTP 422 immediately.
  Also applied to GET /diagnose/history/{pod_name}.

- MEDIUM (DoS): Add 512 KB hard limit on yaml_content in YamlScanRequest
  via a Pydantic field_validator to prevent oversized payload attacks.

- MEDIUM (Gemini key): GeminiAnalyzer now raises EnvironmentError at
  construction if GEMINI_API_KEY is empty, preventing silent
  misconfiguration of the Google Generative AI client.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(api): handle empty SENDSPIN_PORT in config save (#57)

* fix(lxc): fix PulseAudio boot failures on Ubuntu 24.04

Three issues found during LXC deployment:

1. pulseaudio-system.service: Remove User=pulse/Group=pulse — PA
   --system must start as root and drops privileges internally.
   Running as pulse user causes 'System mode refused for non-root user'.

2. pulse-daemon.conf: Replace deprecated enable-lfe-remixing with
   remixing-produce-lfe and remixing-consume-lfe (PulseAudio 17+).

3. install.sh: Add tmpfiles.d entry for /var/run/pulse so the
   directory is recreated on boot (tmpfs is cleared on reboot).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(api): handle empty string values for numeric config fields

The web UI sends numeric fields (SENDSPIN_PORT, PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC) as
empty strings when left blank. This caused:

1. POST /api/config returned 400 because int('') raises ValueError
2. App crashed on startup (ValueError) if config.json contained ''

Fix: use `config.get(key) or default` pattern so empty strings fall
back to defaults, same as missing keys. Also add VOLUME_VIA_MA to the
POST whitelist so the setting is persisted.

Affected fields:
- SENDSPIN_PORT (api.py validation + sendspin_client.py + HA sync)
- PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC (sendspin_client.py + HA sync)
- BT_CHECK_INTERVAL (sendspin_client.py + HA sync)
- BT_MAX_RECONNECT_FAILS (sendspin_client.py + HA sync)
- VOLUME_VIA_MA (missing from POST whitelist)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
add Poetry grapher (#14362)

* Initial plan

* Add Poetry dependency grapher for Python ecosystem

Create a DependencyGrapher subclass for the Poetry package manager that:
- Parses poetry.lock to extract dependency relationships
- Handles the Poetry TOML lockfile format where dependencies are
  a hash of name to version constraint
- Falls back to empty relationships when no lockfile is present
- Registers as the grapher for the 'pip' package manager

Includes test fixtures and specs following the UV grapher PR pattern.

Co-authored-by: jakecoffman <886768+jakecoffman@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add explicit require for NameNormaliser in dependency grapher

Co-authored-by: jakecoffman <886768+jakecoffman@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add poetry show --tree fallback to Poetry dependency grapher

When poetry.lock is missing or fails to parse, fall back to running
`poetry show --tree` to extract dependency relationships, mirroring
the UV grapher's fallback to `uv tree`.

Also adds `run_in_parsed_context` to Python FileParser to enable
running shell commands in a temporary directory with dependency files.

Co-authored-by: jakecoffman <886768+jakecoffman@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: extract TREE_DEPTH_SEGMENT constant, simplify slice

- Extract duplicate regex pattern into TREE_DEPTH_SEGMENT constant
- Use endless range syntax for array truncation

Co-authored-by: jakecoffman <886768+jakecoffman@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard poetry show --tree behind Poetry project detection

Only attempt `poetry show --tree` when the project has [tool.poetry]
in pyproject.toml or uses poetry.core build backend. Non-Poetry
projects (plain pip, pipenv, pip-compile) now return empty
relationships instead of failing.

Co-authored-by: jakecoffman <886768+jakecoffman@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify Python dependency grapher to match Go modules pattern

Remove poetry show --tree fallback, tree parsing code, and
poetry_project? detection. Just parse poetry.lock if present,
otherwise return empty relationships. Non-Poetry projects are
handled naturally since they won't have a poetry.lock.

Co-authored-by: jakecoffman <886768+jakecoffman@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Sorbet type error and failing test in Python dependency grapher

Fix T.must wrapping (must unwrap nilable poetry_lock before accessing
content). Fix corrupt lockfile test to expect DependencyFileNotParseable
since parser validation happens before grapher relationship extraction.

Co-authored-by: jakecoffman <886768+jakecoffman@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback: TOML error handling, path construction, Set optimization

- Rescue TomlRB::ParseError/ValueOverwriteError specifically and re-raise
  as DependencyFileNotParseable (matching existing Python ecosystem pattern)
- Fix run_in_parsed_context to use File.join(file.directory, file.name)
  for correct subdirectory support
- Memoize dependency names as a Set for O(1) lookups in fetch_subdependencies

Co-authored-by: jakecoffman <886768+jakecoffman@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard against non-Hash dependencies in poetry.lock parsing

Address PR review: pkg["dependencies"] could be a String/Array in
malformed lockfiles. Add explicit .is_a?(Hash) guard instead of
relying on || {} which only handles nil.

Co-authored-by: jakecoffman <886768+jakecoffman@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove unused run_in_parsed_context from Python file parser

commit.message:
fix: use direct Harvester Steve API for upload, not Rancher cluster proxy

The ?action=upload endpoint is registered on Harvester's own Steve API
server (https://host/v1/...). When accessed through Rancher's cluster
proxy (https://host/k8s/clusters/local/v1/...) the action is not
forwarded, returning HTTP 403 "no such action upload".

Fix: extract just the https://host portion of the kubeconfig server URL
for the upload call. Status-check GETs continue to use the full server
URL (both paths return the resource data correctly).

harvester-vm.sh:
  upload_base=$(awk -F'/' '{print $1"//"$3}' <<<"$server")
  passes $upload_base to the Python3 upload instead of $server.

image/main.tf:
  harvester_server = regex("^https://[^/]+", trimsuffix(server, ":6443"))
  strips both the :6443 port and any /k8s/clusters/... path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: fix pipeline failing on duplicate NuGet package push (#269)

Re-runs of the CI workflow on the same commit/tag produce the same
effective NuGet version because NuGet ignores SemVer build metadata
(`+N`) when comparing versions, causing a 409 Conflict on the second
push.

## Changes

- **`--skip-duplicate`** added to `dotnet nuget push` in `ci.yml` —
silently skips already-published versions instead of failing
- **`MINVERBUILDMETADATA` syntax fixed** — changed
`build.$GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT` (unexpanded shell variable reference) to
`build.${{ github.run_attempt }}` (valid GitHub Actions expression)

```yaml
# Before
MINVERBUILDMETADATA: build.$GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT
run: dotnet nuget push "./artifacts/*.nupkg" --source "github" --api-key ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

# After
MINVERBUILDMETADATA: build.${{ github.run_attempt }}
run: dotnet nuget push "./artifacts/*.nupkg" --source "github" --api-key ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} --skip-duplicate
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->

commit.message:
Fix type errors and add CI linting with pre-commit hooks (#10)

This pull request introduces a robust linting and formatting workflow
for the project, adds npm-based pre-commit hooks using Husky, and
improves type safety and clarity across the codebase and tests. The most
important changes are summarized below.

**CI/CD and Development Workflow Improvements**

* Added a `lint` job to `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that runs Ruff (lint
and format checks) and Pyright for type checking, ensuring code quality
in CI.
* Introduced `package.json` with Husky as a dev dependency, and scripts
for pre-commit linting and formatting checks.
* Added `.husky/pre-commit` script to activate the Python environment
and run Ruff and Pyright checks automatically on each commit.
* Updated `README.md` with instructions for npm-based pre-commit hooks,
linting, and formatting, making it easier for contributors to follow the
new workflow.

commit.message:
Update locale strings (#8201)

This PR updates the locale translation strings extracted from source
code.

## Triggered By
Commit: `6253c9ab1200a04c226e5f7d2a6ac726e354ca4f`
Message: refactor: complete middleware consolidation (phase 3) —
remaining entity middleware and inline loads (#8176)

Completes the middleware consolidation started in #8166/#8169 by
refactoring all five remaining middleware classes to extend
`AbstractEntityMiddleware` and eliminating all identified inline entity
loads from route handlers.

## Middleware refactors

- **`FamilyMiddleware`** / **`PersonMiddleware`** — straightforward
extends; removes manual `MiddlewareInterface` boilerplate and legacy
`withStatus(412, ...)` error responses
- **`PersonMiddleware`** gains an optional `$routeParamName` constructor
param (default `'personId'`) to support routes whose path param is named
differently (e.g. `{userID}`)
- **`EventsMiddleware`** — standardises missing-param response to
**412** via `renderErrorJSON` (was a bespoke 400 via `renderJSON`)
- **`PropertyMiddleware`** — extends `AbstractEntityMiddleware`; the
constructor-injected `$type` filter is applied inside `loadEntity()`: if
the loaded property's class doesn't match, `null` is returned and the
base class returns a standard 404. No `process()` override needed.
- **`UserMiddleware`** — extends `AbstractEntityMiddleware` with a
`process()` override that preserves the auth logic: current-user
identity shortcut sets the attribute directly from the in-memory user;
non-admin requesting another user's data gets a 401; admin path
delegates to `parent::process()` for the DB lookup + 404 guard.

```php
// UserMiddleware — auth logic preserved, entity load delegated to parent
public function process(ServerRequestInterface $request, RequestHandlerInterface $handler): ResponseInterface
{
    $loggedInUser = AuthenticationManager::getCurrentUser();
    if ($loggedInUser->getId() == $userId) {
        return $handler->handle($request->withAttribute('user', $loggedInUser)); // shortcut
    }
    if (!$loggedInUser->isAdmin()) {
        return $response->withStatus(401);
    }
    return parent::process($request, $handler); // loads from DB + 404 guard
}
```

## Inline entity load fixes

| Route | Was | Now |
|---|---|---|
| `GET /groups/{groupID}/roles` |
`GroupQuery::create()->findOneById($groupID)` |
`$request->getAttribute('group')` + `GroupMiddleware` |
| `DELETE /groups/{groupID}/removeperson/{userID}` |
`PersonQuery::create()->findPk($args['userID'])` |
`$request->getAttribute('person')` + `new PersonMiddleware('userID')` |
| `POST /groups/{groupID}/addperson/{userID}` | same as above | same fix
|
| `GET /api/user/{userId}/permissions` |
`UserQuery::create()->findPk($userId)` (redundant — `UserMiddleware`
already on the group) | `$request->getAttribute('user')` |

Removes now-unused `PersonQuery` import from `people-groups.php` and
`UserQuery` import from `user-admin.php`.

commit.message:
test: add comprehensive visual rendering integration tests

Add 29 structural snapshot tests covering all markdown features:
headings, inline styles, links, blockquotes, lists, code blocks,
tables, horizontal rules, images, mixed content, smart punctuation,
viewport culling, and bundled document rendering.

Each test renders markdown headlessly through egui and verifies
block structure, heights, positions, span coverage, and style flags.
Tests are paired with reference .md files in test-assets/visual-refs/
for visual verification by humans and agents.

Also re-exports parse types (Block, StyledText, Alignment, etc.) and
cache fields (blocks, heights, cum_y) for integration test access.

Fixes diagnostic test issues from previous agents (clippy lints).

fix: add missing `properties` field to object-type JSON Schemas in MCP tool definitions

The Copilot API (CAPI) validator rejects tool schemas where nested object-type
properties lack a `properties` field — even when `additionalProperties` is present.
This caused `CAPIError: 400 Invalid schema` at registration time.

Fixed:
- `http_request.headers`: was `{type: "object"}` without `properties`
- `create_run_configuration.env` / `edit_run_configuration.env`: same issue
- Generalized `addEnvProperty()` → `addDictProperty(tool, name, desc)` helper
  that always includes `properties: {}` + `additionalProperties: {type: "string"}`

Added `testAllToolSchemasAreValid()` — validates every tool's inputSchema:
- Root schema has `type`, `properties`, `required`
- Every property has `type` and `description`
- Object-type properties must have `properties` field
- Array-type properties must have `items` field

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
